description_long: This is a two-story residential house with a tan exterior and white trim, featuring a complex, multi-sectioned hip roof in gray. The structure includes a prominent bay window section on one side, adding architectural interest and extra interior space. Windows are arranged in symmetrical rows, with white frames and horizontal divisions, providing a balanced and orderly appearance. The main entryway is located at the rear, accessed by a small set of steps and flanked by two windows, with a simple white door. The house sits on a flat, light-colored base, and is surrounded by minimal landscaping, including a few tall, narrow shrubs. The overall design is practical and suburban, with a focus on functionality and modest decorative elements.
description: A two-story tan house with a gray hip roof, white trim, and symmetrical window rows, featuring a bay window section and a simple rear entryway.
description_view_0: This view shows the side of the house with the bay window section and a clear view of the hip roof's angles.
description_view_1: This view highlights the long side of the house with symmetrical window rows and the bay window protruding on the left.
description_view_2: This view displays the rear of the house, featuring the main entryway with steps, flanked by windows and some landscaping.
description_view_3: This view shows the opposite side, with the bay window section and additional landscaping visible, as well as the roof's complex geometry.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows with bay window section
attribute_entryway_design: Simple rear entry with white door, small steps, and minimal decorative elements
